content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(0, 2, 0), (2, 6, 1), (1, 8, 2), (0, 3, 3), (1, 7, 12), (7, 8, 12), (5, 9, 13), (2, 3, 16), (2, 8, 19), (4, 5, 21), (3, 8, 23), (3, 5, 25), (4, 9, 26)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 5 exactly at time 21.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

For time 21:

* Edge (0, 2, 0) has node 0 and time 0. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 6, 1) has node 2 and time 1. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (1, 8, 2) has node 1 and time 2. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (0, 3, 3) has node 0 and time 3. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 7, 12) has node 1 and time 12. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (7, 8, 12) has node 7 and time 12. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (5, 9, 13) has node 5 and time 13. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (2, 3, 16) has node 2 and time 16. -> Add node 3.
* Edge (2, 8, 19) has node 2 and time 19. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (4, 5, 21) has node 4 and time 21. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (3, 8, 23) has node 3 and time 23. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (3, 5, 25) has node 3 and time 25. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (4, 9, 26) has node 4 and time 26. -> Add node 9.

The nodes matched at time 21 are 6, 8, 9, and 23.

Answer: [6, 8, 9, 23]
